text.title: Thumbs Down → Eval — Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the shape of an `EvalCandidate` (intermediate) and an admitted eval-suite row.

list.item: `scrub_passed` — boolean (true only if no entity remained)
`judge_votes` — array of 3 `{judge_id, wrong, representative, reproducible, model}`
`expected_behavior` — string (NOT a hard expected output; behaviour assertion e.g. "should refuse to invent records")
`assertion_type` — enum {refuses, retrieves, abstains, structured-output, no-pii, citation-present}

antipattern.reason: Violates r2 PII gate. Rejection is non-negotiable.

antipattern.description: Admitted rows raw prompt contains entity-tagged tokens like `[PER_1]`'s value (e.g. literal "John") leaking through.
antipattern.reason: Indicates scrub did not actually redact; PII contamination of eval suite.
Violates r4 cap. Surplus must queue.

antipattern.description: Admitted row sets `expected_behavior` as an exact string output ("must return: 'I cannot help'").
antipattern.reason: Brittle assertions overfit to wording. Use behaviour assertions (refuses, retrieves, abstains) instead.

list.item: Shorten prompts > 4k tokens to first 1.5k + "[...]" + last 500 for storage.
Coalesce identical clustered failures into one row with `frequency_count` field.
